source_docs.title: scifi-ATAC-seq: massive-scale single-cell chromatin accessibility sequencing using combinatorial fluidic indexing

oligos.name: Tn5-ME-A index adapter (A1–A12)
oligos.role: Barcoded Tn5 transferred (s5) adapter; carries custom Read1 handle + 5-bp inline index + mosaic end
oligos.notes: 12 distinct 5-bp barcodes (A1–A12) replace NNNNN; actual barcode sequences are in Additional file 2: Table S1 (not included). 5' 14 nt (TCGTCGGCAGCGTC) is the standard Nextera Read1 prefix retained for 10x compatibility; GATATGTGATAATGAGGAC is the custom Read1 priming region; AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG is the 19-bp mosaic end.

oligos.name: Tn5-ME-B index adapter (B1–B8)
oligos.role: Barcoded Tn5 transferred (s7) adapter; carries custom Read2 handle + 5-bp inline index + mosaic end
oligos.notes: 8 distinct 5-bp barcodes (B1–B8) replace NNNNN; sequences in Additional file 2: Table S1 (not included). 5' 15 nt (GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG) is the standard Nextera S7/Read2 prefix retained for 10x SI-PCR compatibility; TGAATGTGTAGAAGACAGA is the custom Read2 priming region; AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG is the mosaic end.

oligos.name: Tn5-ME-Rev
oligos.role: Common 5'-phosphorylated mosaic-end complement oligo annealed to both Tn5-ME-A and Tn5-ME-B
oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated (printed in source as 3'-TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC-phos-5'). 19-bp reverse complement of the mosaic end.

oligos.name: Annealed Tn5-ME-A adapter duplex
oligos.role: Double-stranded transposon adapter loaded onto Tn5 (row index)
oligos.notes: Annealed 1:1 at 100 µM; only the 19-bp mosaic-end region is duplexed, leaving the handle+barcode as a 5' single-stranded flap.

oligos.name: Annealed Tn5-ME-B adapter duplex
oligos.role: Double-stranded transposon adapter loaded onto Tn5 (column index)
oligos.notes: Tn5-ME-A and Tn5-ME-B duplexes mixed 1:1 and loaded onto Tn5 to build the two-sided barcoded transposome.

oligos.name: scifi-qPCR-F
oligos.role: qPCR primer to determine library amplification cycles (forward, anneals to Read1/s5 handle)
oligos.notes: Identical to the Tn5-ME-A handle and the Read1 sequencing primer; used with 2x Luna qPCR master mix.

oligos.name: scifi-qPCR-R
oligos.role: qPCR primer to determine library amplification cycles (reverse, anneals to Read2/s7 handle)
oligos.notes: Identical to the Tn5-ME-B handle and the Read2 sequencing primer.

oligos.name: Custom Read1 sequencing primer (1_Read1)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified, 100 µM. Anneals to the Tn5-ME-A handle; Read1 then reads the inline 5-bp A barcode, mosaic end, then genomic DNA.

oligos.name: Custom Index1 (i7) sequencing primer (2_Index1)
oligos.role: Custom i7 index-read primer (reads 8-bp sample index)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified, 100 µM. Reverse complement of the Tn5-ME-B handle; primes the i7 sample-index read.

oligos.name: Custom Index2 (i5) sequencing primer (3_Index2)
oligos.role: Custom i5 index-read primer (reads 16-bp 10x cell barcode)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified, 100 µM. Anneals within the Read1/s5 handle region and reads the 16-bp 10x gel-bead barcode as index2.

oligos.name: Custom Read2 sequencing primer (4_Read2)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified, 100 µM. Anneals to the Tn5-ME-B handle; Read2 reads the inline 5-bp B barcode, mosaic end, then genomic DNA.

oligos.name: 10x Chromium scATAC gel-bead barcoded oligo
oligos.role: Provides P5 + 16-bp cell barcode; primes on the Tn5-ME-A handle during GEM incubation

components.name: P5
components.role: Illumina P5 (10x kit constant; not printed in source)

components.name: 16-bp cell barcode
components.role: 10x GEM gel-bead cell barcode (read as i5)

oligos.notes: From the Chromium Single Cell ATAC v1.1 (Next GEM) kit (CG000209). Exact bead sequence not printed in the source; P5 and Nextera Read1 partial shown as standard 10x constants, barcode as placeholder.

oligos.name: 10x Chromium i7 sample-index (SI-PCR) primer
oligos.role: Adds 8-bp i7 sample index and P7 during library amplification

components.name: P7
components.role: Illumina P7 (10x kit constant; not printed in source)

components.name: S7/Read2 prefix
components.role: anneals to the Tn5-ME-B handle prefix

oligos.notes: From the Chromium Single Cell ATAC v1.1 kit. Exact primer/index sequences not printed in the source; P7 and S7 prefix shown as standard 10x constants, index as placeholder.

final_library.source_label: scifi-ATAC-seq final library (Chromium scATAC v1.1 + two-sided barcoded Tn5)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence:
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [CELL_BARCODE:16] TCGTCGGCAGCGTCGATATGTGATAATGAGGAC [BARCODE:5] AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT [BARCODE:5] TCTGTCTTCTACACATTCACCGAGCCCACGAGAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
|--- P5 ---|--- 10x cell BC (i5) ---|--- Read1 / Tn5-ME-A handle ---|-Tn5 A idx-|-- ME --|-- genomic insert --|-- ME(rc) --|-Tn5 B idx-|--- Read2 / Tn5-ME-B handle (rc) ---|-- i7 --|-- P7(rc) --|
annotation_lines: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC — Illumina P5 (from 10x kit; not printed in source)
[CELL_BARCODE:16] — 10x GEM gel-bead cell barcode, read as Index2 (i5)
[BARCODE:5] — Tn5-ME-A inline index (s5 end; 12 variants, Table S1)
[BARCODE:5] — Tn5-ME-B inline index (s7 end; 8 variants, reverse complement)
TCTGTCTTCTACACATTCACCGAGCCCACGAGAC — Read2 handle = Tn5-ME-B handle (reverse complement)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:8] — i7 sample index (from 10x kit), read as Index1 (i7)
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG — Illumina P7 (from 10x kit; not printed in source)

evidence.locator: scifi-ATAC-seq final library (Chromium scATAC v1.1 + two-sided barcoded Tn5)

library_generation.title: Anneal indexed Tn5 adapters
library_generation.summary: Each barcoded Tn5-ME-A (A1–A12) and Tn5-ME-B (B1–B8) oligo is annealed to the common 5'-phosphorylated Tn5-ME-Rev to form double-stranded transposon adapters.
library_generation.note: Oligos are mixed 1:1 in TE at 100 µM, heated to 98 °C, and cooled to 25 °C at -1 °C per 10 s. Only the 19-bp mosaic end duplexes; the handle + 5-bp barcode remain a single-stranded 5' flap.
library_generation.product:
5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCGATATGTGATAATGAGGAC NNNNN AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'
                                          3'-TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC-5'p
(NNNNN = 5-bp Tn5-ME-A index; Tn5-ME-B assembled identically with its own handle/index)

library_generation.title: Assemble 96 two-sided barcoded transposomes
library_generation.summary: Tn5 is loaded with the annealed A and B adapters; a row A-index and a column B-index are combined in each of 96 wells to make uniquely barcoded transposomes.
library_generation.note: A-adapter/Tn5 and B-adapter/Tn5 are assembled separately (25 °C, 1 h), then 1.5 µL of an A row and 1.5 µL of a B column are dispensed per well so every well holds a distinct A×B combination (12×8 = 96).
library_generation.product:
[Tn5 monomer]==Tn5-ME-A adapter (handle-A + BC-A + ME)
[Tn5 monomer]==Tn5-ME-B adapter (handle-B + BC-B + ME)

library_generation.title: Indexed tagmentation of nuclei
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are distributed into the 96 wells and tagmented, inserting the barcoded mosaic-end adapters into accessible chromatin so each fragment gains an inline A barcode on one end and a B barcode on the other.
library_generation.note: 10 µL nuclei per well (37 °C, 60 min), quenched with EDTA stop buffer. Represented after gap repair as a single sense strand:

library_generation.title: GEM cell-barcoding on 10x Chromium
library_generation.summary: Pooled tagmented nuclei are overloaded onto the Chromium scATAC v1.1 platform; inside each GEM the gel-bead oligo primes the Tn5-ME-A handle and adds P5 and the 16-bp cell barcode.
library_generation.note: Follows 10x CG000209. ~100–400k nuclei are loaded (vs the recommended 15.3k), relying on the inline Tn5 barcodes to resolve multiple nuclei per droplet.

library_generation.title: Library amplification (SI-PCR)
library_generation.summary: qPCR sets the cycle number, then the sample is amplified with 10x P5 and i7 sample-index/P7 primers to complete the sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: qPCR with scifi-qPCR-F/R defines cycles (~Cq, ≤9 extra cycles, target ~100 ng). SI-PCR adds the 8-bp i7 sample index and P7 to the s7/Tn5-ME-B end.

library_sequencing.note: Custom 1_Read1 primer (identical to the Tn5-ME-A handle) anneals to the bottom strand; reads the inline 5-bp Tn5-A barcode, the 19-bp mosaic end, then genomic DNA. Read length not stated in source; 50 shown as the 10x scATAC v1.1 default. Inline Tn5 barcode is demultiplexed with cutadapt.
library_sequencing.diagram:
TCGTCGGCAGCGTCGATATGTGATAATGAGGAC------->  (1_Read1)
5'-...ACAC[CB:16]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCGATATGTGATAATGAGGAC[BC-A:5]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[gDNA]...-3'
3'-...TGTG[CB:16]AGCAGCCGTCGCAGCTATACACTATTACTCCTG[BC-A:5]TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[gDNA]...-5'
   template = bottom strand;  read = [BC-A:5] + mosaic end (19 bp) + genomic insert

library_sequencing.note: Custom 2_Index1 primer (the reverse complement of the Tn5-ME-B handle, i.e. identical to the top-strand Read2-handle segment) anneals to the bottom strand and reads the 8-bp i7 sample index.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'-...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[BC-B:5]TCTGTCTTCTACACATTCACCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:8]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
3'-...GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGA[BC-B:5]AGACAGAAGATGTGTAAGTGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:8]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'
                                 TCTGTCTTCTACACATTCACCGAGCCCACGAGAC------->  (2_Index1)
   template = bottom strand;  read = 8-bp i7 sample index

library_sequencing.note: Custom 3_Index2 primer (the reverse complement of the Tn5-ME-A / Read1 handle) anneals to the top-strand handle and extends back toward P5, reading the 16-bp 10x gel-bead cell barcode as index2 (the 'i5 beads barcode'; extracted with a UMI-tools 16-N bc-pattern).
library_sequencing.diagram:
3'-AGCAGCCGTCGCAGCTATACACTATTACTCCTG-5'  (3_Index2, 5'->3' = GTCCTCATTATCACATATCGACGCTGCCGACGA)
              <-------[reads CB:16]
5'-...GAGATCTACAC[CB:16]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCGATATGTGATAATGAGGAC-3'
3'-...CTCTAGATGTG[CB:16]AGCAGCCGTCGCAGCTATACACTATTACTCCTG-5'
   template = top strand;  read = 16-bp 10x cell barcode

library_sequencing.note: Custom 4_Read2 primer (the Tn5-ME-B handle) anneals to the top-strand Read2-handle segment and extends toward the insert, reading the inline 5-bp Tn5-B barcode, the 19-bp mosaic end, then genomic DNA on the opposite fragment end. Read length not stated; 50 shown as the 10x default.
library_sequencing.diagram:
AGACAGAAGATGTGTAAGTGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG  (4_Read2, 5'->3' = GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGTGAATGTGTAGAAGACAGA)
   <-------[reads BC-B:5 + mosaic end + genomic]
5'-...[gDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[BC-B:5]TCTGTCTTCTACACATTCACCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:8]...-3'
3'-...[gDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGA[BC-B:5]AGACAGAAGATGTGTAAGTGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:8]...-5'
   template = top strand;  read = [BC-B:5] + mosaic end (19 bp) + genomic insert

title: scifi-ATAC-seq
description: scifi-ATAC-seq (single-cell combinatorial fluidic indexing ATAC-seq) profiles genome-wide chromatin accessibility in single nuclei at massive scale. Nuclei are pre-indexed in a 96-well plate with a two-sided barcoded Tn5 transposase (12 Tn5-ME-A × 8 Tn5-ME-B = 96 inline 5-bp barcode combinations), then ~100–300k nuclei are overloaded onto the 10x Genomics Chromium scATAC v1.1 (Next GEM) platform, which adds a 16-bp gel-bead cell barcode. Because each fragment carries an inline 5-bp Tn5 barcode on both the s5 and s7 ends plus the 16-bp 10x barcode, the combined identifier resolves nuclei even when several occupy the same droplet, greatly reducing barcode collisions from overloading. Paired genomic reads map Tn5 integration (open-chromatin) sites; the inline Tn5 barcodes are read at the start of Read1/Read2 and the 10x barcode is read as the i5 index.

reference.label: scifi-ATAC-seq: massive-scale single-cell chromatin accessibility sequencing using combinatorial fluidic indexing

original_publication.title: scifi-ATAC-seq: massive-scale single-cell chromatin accessibility sequencing using combinatorial fluidic indexing

authors.name: Xuan Zhang
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics, University of Georgia, Athens, GA, USA

authors.name: Alexandre P. Marand
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics, University of Georgia, Athens, GA, USA; current address: Department of Molecular, Cellular, and Developmental Biology, University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI, USA

authors.name: Haidong Yan
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics, University of Georgia, Athens, GA, USA

authors.name: Robert J. Schmitz
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics, University of Georgia, Athens, GA, USA

throughput.summary: Up to ~200,000 nuclei across multiple samples indexed in a single emulsion reaction; ~20-fold (up to 27-fold) throughput increase over standard 10x scATAC-seq. Recovered ~35k and ~70k clean nuclei from 100k and 200k inputs; an 8-sample/7-genotype 300k-input run recovered 199,212 high-quality nuclei and 133,524 singlet clean nuclei.
throughput.cells: ~35,000 (100k input) and ~69,302 (200k input) clean nuclei per channel; 133,524 singlet clean nuclei from a 300k 8-sample run
throughput.dna: Sequenced to ~7,617 read pairs per cell (average unique-read rate 62.3%); target ~100 ng library DNA

publication.statistical_model: Genotype/barcode-collision assignment models allele counts as a binomial distribution with Bayesian posterior probabilities (via Souporcell); nuclei-per-droplet and barcode-collision rates estimated with a probabilistic droplet-occupancy model.

other.label: Platform / kit
other.value: 10x Genomics Chromium, Single Cell ATAC v1.1 (Next GEM) kit (CG000209); sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq 6000 S4, dual-index, custom sequencing primers

other.label: Tn5 source
other.value: HS-Tn5 expressed/purified per Tu et al. 2020; plasmid from Addgene #127916

other.label: Sample / species
other.value: Zea mays (maize) seedling nuclei (B73, Mo17 and 7 genotypes); also applied to soybean

other.label: Data availability
other.value: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA996051; code at github.com/schmitzlab/scifi-ATAC-seq and Zenodo record 10891860

other.label: Cross-cell contamination
other.value: 1.47% and 1.69% for scifi-ATAC-seq vs 5.63% for standard 16k scATAC-seq, aided by the double-sided barcode design

data_processing.summary: Paired-end reads are demultiplexed by the 16-bp 10x bead barcode and the inline Tn5 barcodes, aligned to the maize v5 genome, deduplicated per cell barcode, and reduced to single-base Tn5 integration sites; nuclei are then called and QC'd, clustered and annotated, and genotype-based modeling is used to detect barcode collisions and index-hopping contamination.
data_processing.statistical_model: Binomial model of allele counts with Bayesian posterior for genotype/collision assignment (Souporcell); TF-IDF + SVD for accessibility dimensionality reduction
